steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $6ab$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: $12ab$ and $6ab$ have the same variable factor, $ab$, so subtract only the coefficients: $$12ab-6ab=(12-6)ab=6ab.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The equivalent expression is $6ab$, which matches the correct answer.

steps.title: Keep the variable factor
steps.content: Because both terms include the same factor $ab$, the result must still include $ab$. The other choices drop the variables, and forgetting that $12-6=6$.
